events.title: One killed, five wounded in terror attacks in Israel
events.description: At least one person was killed and five others wounded in multiple shootings in Israel on Sunday, and security forces killed an attacker identified as a Palestinian citizen of Israel. Israel's emergency service Magen David Adom said a man aged around 35 had died of gunshot wounds. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports.
events.ticker_label: 💥 One killed, five wounded in terror attacks in Israel

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
events.description: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rain Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders. Three-hourly rainfall totals on Wednesday reached 105mm in Chiyoda, Tokyo, which was a record high for the month. Sustained wind speeds of 80mph (130kph) were recorded on Monday – making it a category 1 typhoon – bringing damage and disruption to businesses, transport, infrastructure and the environment. By Wednesday, 23 people had been injured, 17 of whom were in Okinawa. The typhoon damaged 57 homes and led to 60,000 homes losing electricity. In addition to this, 1.52 million people were advised to evacuate by authorities. The typhoon damaged the exterior wall of Himeji Castle, a Unesco world heritage site in western Japan. The maximum recorded wind speed at Himeji was 56mph, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. The typhoon has now weakened into a tropical depression and has moved eastwards, away from the islands. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan

events.title: Middle East live: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after ceasefire agreement
events.description: Israel on Sunday struck "terrorist" targets in Beirut's southern suburbs in retaliation for Hezbollah's firing towards northern Israel, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said. The strikes came days after a fragile ceasefire between the Israeli and Lebanese governments, negotiated by the US, came into effect. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after ceasefire agreement

events.title: Aviation industry looks skywards as leaders fly in for Rio summit
events.description: Oil tankers may be stuck behind strait of Hormuz, but holding the Iata AGM in Brazil defies warnings of impending shortages Nothing says jet fuel crisis, as one prospective attender put it, like flying everyone to Rio de Janeiro. Aviation leaders will converge in Brazil this weekend for the Iata AGM, the annual global airline summit, with the industry still, for the most part, looking resolutely skyward. The oil tankers may still be stuck behind the strait of Hormuz as the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran flickers on, but for now, airlines continue to defy dire warnings of impending shortages which had stoked fears of a summer of chaos for European holidaymakers. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Family values’ African charter condemned by rights groups as regressive and dangerous
events.description: Draft treaty claims sexual and reproductive health and rights are an existential threat to the African family An African treaty that rejects longstanding international human rights obligations moved a step closer to becoming policy this week as governments across the continent met in Ghana. The draft African charter on family, sovereignty and values, seen by the Guardian, asserts that African values and culture are under attack from “foreign ideologies” and urges states to withdraw from any agreements that do not align with the principles of the charter, including the 2003 Maputo protocol , which promotes gender equality and protects the reproductive and health rights of women and girls. Continue reading...

events.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response

At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

events.title: Nauru issues rare statement after whistleblower alleges violent threats against Australia’s non-citizens

The unexpected defence, sent shortly after midday on Thursday, was mounted hours after the independent MP Andrew Wilkie used his three-minute constituency statement to read claims from an anonymous whistleblower familiar with the arrangements of the secretive $2.5bn deal between Australia and Nauru. events.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
events.description: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard across the industry and beyond. Some outlets have begun offering perks to customers who bring their own bags, plates or containers. Continue reading...

events.title: Nigeria: Hundreds of kidnapped women, children freed from Boko Haram jihadists

events.title: Dubai luxury hotels court local residents as tourism slumps

events.title: Peru’s discontented voters face straight left-right choice in election runoff
events.description: Keiko Fujimori, the daughter of 1990s leader Alberto, is vying with a congressman to become country’s ninth president in a decade Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday in an election runoff that pits a perennial rightwing candidate, Keiko Fujimori, against a leftist congressman, Roberto Sánchez. Amid rising crime, chronic political instability, corruption scandals and voter apathy, they are vying to become Peru’s ninth president in a decade. Fujimori, who is the daughter of the late president Alberto Fujimori, won 17% of the vote in the first round in April . Sánchez, a former trade and tourism minister, took 12 % of the vote, edging out Rafael López Aliaga, an ultra-conservative former Lima mayor. The stage is set for a polarised left-right replay of the country’s last election in 2021. Continue reading...

events.title: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: When U.S. foreign aid changed, AIDS workers in Africa felt it

events.title: Peru is set to elect its 10th president in a decade
events.description: Peruvians will elect their new president Sunday with polls suggesting a polarized but tight race between hard-right candidate Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez.

events.title: Sweden mulls prison for young teens as violent crime rises
events.description: In the fight against escalating gang crime, Sweden's parliament will vote on plans to hold 13-year-olds criminally responsible and, in serious cases, imprison them. Other European nations are also lowering age limits.

events.title: Pope Leo in Spain: More than a million line Madrid streets
events.description: Huge crowds welcomed Pope Leo XIV to Madrid on Sunday. The pontiff is on a five-day trip around Spain during which he is meeting vulnerable people such as migrants and the homeless, and promoting "genuine human values."

events.title: Peru votes for new president — for ninth time in 10 years
events.description: Peru could soon have its ninth president in 10 years, as voters choose between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sanchez in a runoff election overshadowed by instability and crime.

events.title: India news: CJP demands minister's removal in seven days
events.description: The Cockroach Janta Party has given an ultimatum for the removal of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Meanwhile, the Indonesian foreign minister is visiting Delhi.

events.title: Taiwan deploys vessels in response to Chinese operation
events.description: Taiwan's coast guard confronts Chinese vessels near the island after Beijing launched what it called a "law enforcement operation."

events.title: Ethiopia's election results will likely confirm status quo
events.description: Ethiopia's ruling Prosperity Party is set for a decisive election win when results are announced. The election was marked by security challenges, a divided opposition and the exclusion of entire regions from voting.

events.title: Ukraine hits St. Petersburg again after Putin rejects talks
events.description: Ukrainian drones targeted St. Petersburg and the surrounding region for a second time after Russian President Vladimir Putin rejected Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's offer of peace talks.

events.title: French Open: Andreeva wins first Grand Slam tennis title
events.description: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva beat Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the final in Paris. She is the first Russian woman to win a Grand Slam since Maria Sharapova won the French Open in 2014.

events.title: High-functioning depression: When success masks suffering
events.description: People with depression don't always feel overwhelmed or struggle to get things done — some are highly efficient and productive, despite their low moods. So it can't be that bad, right? Wrong!

events.title: More than 1.2 million people attend Pope Leo's mass in Madrid
events.description: More than 1.2 million people filled the streets of Madrid on Sunday to attend a mass by Pope Leo XIV at which he called for a renewal of the Catholic faith, saying Spaniards should not look at religion as "a museum of the past to be visited, but a school of faith from which to draw even today".

events.title: Armenians vote in parliamentary elections seen as ‘referendum on independence’
events.description: Armenians are voting on Sunday in parliamentary elections as the incumbent government, facing mounting pressure from Russia, seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West. Speaking to residents, FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot said the vote is widely being seen as a "referendum on independence". One voter described the election as "existential" for Armenia, citing aggressive rhetoric from neighbouring countries. Meanwhile, according to Western intelligence and government officials, Russia has stepped up covert efforts to undermine Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan’s bid for re-election next month, fearing that a victory could cement the former Soviet republic's realignment with the West.

events.title: Peru votes for its ninth president in 10 years
events.description: Peruvians will choose on Sunday their ninth president in 10 years, in a tight runoff election between conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sanchez who are trying to woo voters fed up with political chaos and rising crime. Fujimori, daughter of former autocratic president Alberto Fujimori, is making her fourth bid for the presidency.

events.title: Mirra Andreeva claims first Grand Slam title with victory at Roland-Garros
events.description: In the first Grand Slam final of her career, Mirra Andreeva saw off Polish qualifier Maja Chwalińska in under 90 minutes to clinch the French Open title. At 19, the Russian is the youngest woman to win Roland-Garros since Monica Seles in 1992.

events.title: Armenian parliamentary vote will test prime minister's pivot from Russia
events.description: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that will see the party of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, who seeks to lessen dependence on Moscow, facing off against that of billionaire Russian-Armenian businessman Samvel Karapetyan. The Kremlin has been accused of seeking to sway the vote to undermine Armenia's EU ambitions.

events.title: Pope jokes about Bad Bunny and which football teams he supports
events.description: Pope Leo revealed he'll be backing the USA and that he supports Real Madrid as he began a visit to Spain.

events.title: Russian satellites have been causing GPS disruption across Europe, scientists claim
events.description: A network of Russian satellites has caused brief disruptions to GPS signals across Europe on perhaps dozens of occasions since 2019, new research suggests.

events.title: British deputy PM tells Vance he was wrong to blame immigration for teen's murder
events.description: They discussed the case of a student who was handcuffed as he lay dying.

events.title: UK's Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz to discuss support for Ukraine
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is hosting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Sunday
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK's Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz to discuss support for Ukraine

events.title: 1 million turn out for pope's Mass in Spain and iconic procession
events.description: More than one million people have poured into a central Madrid plaza for Pope Leo XIV’s main Mass

events.title: With rising crime on their minds, Peruvians to vote for president yet again
events.description: Peruvians are chosing between two presidential candidates with starkly different views

events.title: Kosovo voters return to the polls after parties fail to agree on a new president
events.description: Voters in Kosovo head to the polls for an early parliamentary election

events.title: Raúl Castro makes first appearance since US charges to celebrate birthday
events.description: He was indicted for alleged role in the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft.

events.title: Senior British royals gather as King Charles' nephew marries nurse Harriet Sperling
events.description: King Charles III, Queen Camilla and other senior British royals were among guests attending the wedding of Charles' nephew, Peter Phillips

events.title: Police arrest supporters of banned group ahead of rally in Pakistan-administered Kashmir
events.description: Police in Pakistan-administered Kashmir have arrested dozens of supporters of an outlawed group demanding improved services and greater rights

events.title: Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit placed on a lung transplant list
events.description: Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit has been placed on a lung transplant list due to worsening pulmonary fibrosis

events.title: Bernadette Chirac, formidable former first lady of France, dies at 93
events.description: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France, has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bernadette Chirac, formidable former first lady of France, dies at 93

events.title: Pope acknowledges stiff competition with Bad Bunny this weekend in Spain
events.description: Pope Leo XIV acknowledges that he's competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend

events.title: ‘The true cost of peace’: UN honours fallen peacekeepers as dangers mount
events.description: The United Nations paused on Friday to pay tribute to the more than 4,500 peacekeepers who lost their lives in the line of duty over the past 78 years.

events.title: Building trust on patrol: One police officer’s peacekeeping journey in South Sudan
events.description: From a police station in Germany to the UN Mission in South Sudan, Stephanie Königs has been talking to UN News about her journey to becoming a peacekeeper – and the importance of trust and empathy in the field.

events.title: Daycare operator arrested after 3-year-old was left unconscious in pool for 20 minutes, died
events.description: Unlicensed Louisiana daycare operator Joann Johnson charged with negligent homicide after 3-year-old boy drowned in backyard pool unsupervised.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Daycare operator arrested after 3-year-old was left unconscious in pool for 20 minutes, died

events.title: At least a dozen shot near festival in Ohio; manhunt for suspects underway
events.description: At least a dozen people were shot near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio, on Saturday and police are actively searching for suspects, authorities said.

events.title: Auburn student Weston Higginbotham found dead in Japan after weeklong search, mom confirms
events.description: James "Weston" Higginbotham, a 20-year-old Auburn University student who vanished during a family vacation in Japan, has been found dead, his mom says.

events.title: Michigan athlete lured by Snapchat message before being shot, dumped in lake while still alive
events.description: In 2018, Emilio Valdez was shot and found in Osmun Lake. After four years, Snapchat evidence helped convict Angel Jose Alvarez of second-degree murder.

events.title: Virginia gun sales spike ahead of July 1 assault weapons ban signed by Gov. Spanberger
events.description: Virginia gun sales surge ahead of July 1 assault weapons ban, with FBI data showing background checks more than doubled compared to last year.

events.title: Passenger allegedly boards flight with fake boarding pass, forcing plane back to gate
events.description: A Houston man allegedly used a fraudulent boarding pass to board a United Airlines flight, causing a three-hour delay before the plane returned to gate.

events.title: NATO's eastern flank races to rearm as Trump pressure exposes Western Europe's defense gap
events.description: Eastern European NATO allies closest to Russia are spending the most on defense, while Western Europe faces pressure to meet the alliance's 5% GDP goal.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV jokes young Spaniards would pick Bad Bunny over him during Madrid visit this weekend
events.description: Pope Leo XIV joked that young Spaniards might choose Bad Bunny over him during his Madrid visit, but an estimated 500,000 gathered for a vigil.

events.title: Jeff Bartos says UN reform is no longer an 'oxymoron' after $570M in cuts
events.description: Jeff Bartos details $570 million in U.N. budget cuts and 2,900 eliminated positions as the Trump administration pushes historic reform efforts.

events.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
events.description: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

events.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
events.description: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.
